text: Prototype Nike shoes with a highly compliant and resilient midsole material combined with a stiff embedded carbon plate reduce the energetic cost of running by approximately 4% compared to established marathon racing shoes.
The 4% energetic cost reduction from carbon-plated super shoes was consistent across all 18 subjects tested, with no subject showing a null or negative effect.
The energetic cost reduction from carbon-plated super shoes is independent of running velocity across the range of 14-18 km/h.
Footwear mass, cushioning, and bending stiffness each independently affect the energetic cost of running.
When shoe mass is controlled for, the combination of a highly compliant/resilient midsole with a stiff embedded plate yields a ~4% energetic advantage over conventional marathon racing flats.
Reducing the energetic cost of running is the most feasible biomechanical path to improving marathon performance at the elite level.
